unit.name: Transition-based dependency parsing
unit.description: Walks through shift, left-arc, and right-arc parsing with a stack and buffer, then explains Nivre's greedy linear-time parsing idea and the older symbolic feature-based version.
unit.summary: The parser used for the assignment is introduced as transition-based parsing, operating over a buffer of unread words, a stack of partial structure, and a set of built arcs [ts=3212s] [ts=3333s]. Using the toy sentence I ate fish, the lecture demonstrates the three core actions: shift moves the next buffer word onto the stack, left-arc makes the top stack item the head of the item below it, and right-arc makes the lower item the head of the top item, popping the dependent after each arc is created [ts=3390s] [ts=3512s]. The key idea is that a sequence of local transition decisions determines the whole parse, and Nivre's contribution was to let machine learning predict each next transition greedily, yielding linear-time parsing instead of more expensive cubic-time context-free parsing methods [ts=3543s] [ts=3687s]. The segment then explains that the pre-neural version used symbolic indicator features over local parser configurations, but conjunctions of words and tags produced millions of sparse features [ts=3764s] [ts=3853s].

key_points.text: A transition-based parser maintains a buffer, a stack, and a set of constructed dependency arcs.
Shift moves the next buffer token to the stack; left-arc and right-arc build dependencies between the top two stack items.
In the I ate fish walkthrough, left-arc makes I depend on ate, and right-arc later makes fish depend on ate and ate depend on ROOT.
Greedy transition prediction gives a linear-time parser because each step predicts one local action.
Older transition parsers relied on symbolic indicator-feature conjunctions that were numerous and sparse.

kp_catalog.name: Transition-based dependency parsing
kp_catalog.description: A parsing strategy that builds a dependency tree through a sequence of local actions such as shift, left-arc, and right-arc over stack-buffer state.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because transition-based dependency parsing clarifies a parsing strategy that builds a dependency tree through a sequence of local actions such as shift, left-arc, and right-arc over stack-buffer state, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Local parser state and features
kp_catalog.description: The representation of a parser decision state using the stack, buffer, nearby words, parts of speech, dependency labels, and other local structural cues.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because local parser state and features clarifies the representation of a parser decision state using the stack, buffer, nearby words, parts of speech, dependency labels, and other local structural cues, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
